result.recit: The matter pits the client (creator of Mistral) against Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre […] who allegedly exploits Quiz Mania by reproducing protected elements. The central issue is imputability: the bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) of 2019 is the cornerstone exhibit but suffers from a BIOS date anomaly […] that undermines its probative weight and exposes it to a formal challenge. In parallel, the client's priority of creation […] has not yet been crystallized by a title or an unassailable dated exhibit, which […] leaves open the defense of independent […] creation. The optimal strategy consists in simultaneously securing three lines of action: (1) redoing or reinforcing the bailiff's report, (2) certifying priority through an INPI filing/Soleau envelope or time-stamped source files, (3) precisely identifying the legal publisher of Tohu-Bohu in order to lock down imputability before any summons (assignation) is served.

hypotheses.si: Cassiopée or Camille Lefèvre appears nowhere in the legal notices of Tohu-Bohu, and a third-party company is designated as publisher.
hypotheses.alors: That third-party company must be joined as a co-defendant, and it must be verified whether Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre is a partner or de facto officer thereof, failing which the action risks dismissal for lack of passive standing (défaut de qualité passive).

hypotheses.si: The client holds a Bastille trademark registration predating 2019 in Class 28 (games) and Class 41 (entertainment).
hypotheses.alors: Trademark infringement becomes the primary theory of liability, enabling an infringement seizure (saisie-contrefaçon) and a preliminary injunction in summary proceedings (référé-interdiction) without having to establish the originality of the work.

hypotheses.si: The BIOS date on the bailiff's report (2009-12-23) is treated as vitiating the instrument.
hypotheses.alors: The entire evidentiary record must be reconstructed by means of a new, procedurally regular bailiff's report, and any acts of infringement predating October 2014 would be time-barred (art. L.335-5 CPI, five-year statute of limitations).

next_steps.label: New bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) covering the […] with verification of the system date.
next_steps.reason: To close the principal evidentiary gap (BIOS date 2009) and establish imputability to Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre in an unassailable manner before any summons (assignation) is served.
next_steps.target: Carto Royale 2 / […] Play — fiche Tohu-Bohu : mentions légales, éditeur, CGU, captures horodatées

next_steps.label: INPI search: trademarks and patents filed by the client and by Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre.
next_steps.reason: To open or close the trademark-infringement theory (the fastest route to a preliminary injunction in summary proceedings (référé-interdiction)) and to detect any opportunistic filing by the defendant subsequent to the client's […].
next_steps.target: Base Marques INPI — signes Bastille, Tohu-Bohu, classes 28 et 41 ; base Brevets pour tout dépôt logiciel

next_steps.label: […] to the client: priority of creation and pre-contractual relationship with Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre.
next_steps.reason: To simultaneously establish the priority of […] (a condition of infringement) and the existence of a prior commercial relationship (a condition of abrupt termination of commercial relations and evidence of intent for purposes of parasitism/unfair competition).
next_steps.target: Client — collecte de : fichiers sources datés, dépôt […]/enveloppe Soleau, tout échange avec Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre (e-mail, […], devis)

cite: Pièce n°5 - Constat d'huissier Site Internet Bastille - 02-10-2019.pdf
faits_pivots.fait: Absence of a certified capture of the legal notices of Quiz Mania designating Cassiopée or Camille Lefèvre as publisher/operator.
faits_pivots.effet: Without direct and dated attribution, all offensive theories (copyright infringement, unfair competition, parasitism) fail for lack of a defendant identified as the actor of the infringing act.
faits_pivots.pourquoi: In copyright infringement (L.335-2 CPI) and software infringement (L.335-3 CPI), the burden of proving imputability rests on the […]; the absence of a certified legal notice breaks the chain of […] between the act of exploitation and the defendant, rendering the […] effectively inadmissible in practice.
faits_pivots.comment_etablir: Emergency bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) covering the […] (Carto Royale 2 / […] Play) capturing the legal notices, terms of use, and identity of the publisher of Tohu-Bohu, supplemented by a formal interrogatory notice (sommation interpellative) to Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre requiring them to declare or deny that they are the publisher.

cite: Pièce n°5 - Constat d'huissier Site Internet Bastille - 02-10-2019.pdf
Pièce n°28 - Cass., Ch. Mixte,6 septembre 2002.pdf
faits_pivots.fait: The BIOS date of the computer used for the bailiff's report was set to 2009-12-23, a date prior to the apparent launch of the product at issue.
faits_pivots.effet: Should the opposing party exploit the BIOS date to challenge the reliability of the bailiff's report of 02-10-2019, all of the certified screen captures would lose their probative weight, and the five-year statute of limitations (L.335-5 CPI / art. 2224 C.civ.) could be raised with respect to acts predating 2014.
faits_pivots.pourquoi: La Cass. Civ. 2e du 11 janvier 1973 and the Ch. Mixte du 6 septembre 2002 reaffirm that the formal regularity of an instrument is a condition of its probative value; a material dating anomaly weakens the enforceability of the bailiff's report and may ground a challenge of forgery (incident de faux) or a […] motion to void.
faits_pivots.comment_etablir: Request […] from the bailiff a written explanatory note regarding the configuration of his workstation and, if necessary, have a new bailiff's report drawn up on a workstation whose system date has been verified and recorded at the head of the instrument.

cite: Pièce n°10- TGI Paris, 26 novembre 2010, n°01-01522.pdf
faits_pivots.fait: Priority of creation of the […] game/software claimed by the client: original […] date not yet certified by an enforceable dated exhibit.
faits_pivots.effet: Without proof of certain priority, the defendant may claim independent […] creation or contest ownership, thereby neutralizing both the copyright infringement claim and the software infringement claim.
faits_pivots.pourquoi: Originality and priority of creation are cumulative conditions for copyright protection (L.111-1 CPI); in the case of software (L.112-2 13° CPI), proof of the date of […] is all the more essential given that the defendant may assert a parallel […] creation defense.
faits_pivots.comment_etablir: Gather: prior INPI/[…] filing, Soleau envelope, time-stamped source files, […] invoices, dated e-mail exchanges with the […], and witness statements from persons who saw the game before the disputed date.

faits_pivots.fait: Existence of a possible letter or prior commercial relationship […] between the client and Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre (analogous to the […] letter of 05-10-2009 in the case law cited).
faits_pivots.effet: If a contractual or pre-contractual relationship is established, the theory of abrupt termination of an established commercial relationship (L.442-1 II C.com.) or of parasitism (unfair competition by free-riding) becomes more robust, and the infringement claim may be compounded by a contractual breach giving rise to separate damages
faits_pivots.pourquoi: Cass. com. of June 9, 2021 (Exhibit 25) illustrates that the existence of a prior written communication may qualify a relationship as an 'established commercial relationship' and trigger the statutory protection against abrupt termination, while also evidencing the defendant's intent for purposes of the infringement claim
faits_pivots.comment_etablir: Question the client regarding any exchange (e-mail, correspondence, […]) with Cassiopée/Camille Lefèvre; collect email correspondence, minutes of […], quotes, or letters of intent

cite: Pièce n°13 - CA Paris, 17 octobre 2012, Karbone 14 c. Carbone 14.pdf
Pièce n°5 - Constat d'huissier Site Internet Bastille - 02-10-2019.pdf
faits_pivots.fait: Whether or not the client has filed a trademark registration covering the sign Bastille (or the sign in dispute), and the priority date of any such filing relative to the defendant's use
faits_pivots.effet: A valid prior trademark registration opens a trademark infringement cause of action (L.716-1 CPI) […] and is the most direct avenue for obtaining an injunction in référé (summary proceedings), independently of the proof of originality required under copyright law
faits_pivots.pourquoi: Trademark infringement (L.713-2 and L.716-4 CPI) does not require proof of originality but only the priority of the registered title and the similarity of the signs and/or goods and services; CA Paris, Karbone 14 (Exhibit 13) illustrates the strength of a registered title as against a subsequent use
faits_pivots.comment_etablir: Verify the client's trademark portfolio on the INPI Marques database, identify the filing date and the classes covered, and establish the defendant's use of the sign by means of a supplementary bailiff's report (constat d'huissier)
